title: Gel Slice Sample Preparation for Proteomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"tttb","text":"This protocol details steps for gel slice sample preparation for proteomic data acquisition. It was adapted from Shevchenko et al. \"In-gel digestion for mass spectrometric characterization of proteins and proteomes.\"Nature protocols 1.6 (2006): 2856.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":216,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"p8n","text":"Wear proper PPE (gloves, safety goggle, and lab coat), and prepare solvents in a chemical fume hood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3o4qe","text":"Store organic solvents in a flammable storage cabinet when not in use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"81ul2","text":"Discard used solvents and buffers in appropriate waste containers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Wash gel slice with 150 µL, Water in a tube (Eppendorf Centrifuge, Cat.#022363204). Incubate for 00:15:00 then remove liquid.
Wash gel slice with 150 µL, 50 mM AMBIC/ACN and incubate for 00:15:00 then remove liquid. Discard used buffers and solvents in appropriate waste containers.
Add 100 µL, 50 mM AMBIC/ACN and incubate for 00:20:00 then remove liquid.
Add 50 µL, Acetonitrile to cover gel slice. The gel slice will visibly shrink and turn white. Incubate for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 then remove liquid.
Add 50 µL, 10 mM DTT, 25 mM AMBIC and incubate in 56 °C, water bath for 01:00:00 then remove liquid.
Add 50 µL, 55 mM IAA, 25 mM AMBIC and incubate in the dark, at Room temperature for 00:45:00 then remove liquid.
Wash gel slice by adding 50 µL, 50 mM AMBIC/ ACN and incubating for 00:15:00 then remove liquid.
Repeat wash step 9.
Add 50 µL, Acetonitrile to cover gel slice. The gel slice will visibly shrink and turn white. Incubate for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 then remove liquid. .

Add 10 µL, 12.5 ng/ml Trypsin, 50 mM AMBIC and incubate on On ice for 00:30:00 , then remove excess liquid, if necessary. All of the liquid (trypsin) should be absorbed into the gel slice at the end of 30 minutes. The gel slice will look clear/hydrated again. There is usually no liquid leftover because only a small amount of trypsin is added however, if necessary, remove excess trypsin before overnight digestion.

Add 50 millimolar (mM), AMBIC to cover the gel slice. Wait 15-20 minutes to make sure the gel slice is still completely covered in liquid. Sometimes the gel is not completely rehydrated after the trypsin has been absorbed, and it absorbs a lot of the AMBIC if you only use enough to cover. This is especially a problem with larger gel pieces.
Digest the protein(s) at 37 °C Overnight in an incubator or thermocycler.
Remove gel slice from 37 °C incubator or thermocycler.
Add 20 µL, 50 mM AMBIC after tryptic digestion. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00 .
Add 30 µL, 50% ACN/ 5% FA to cover the gel slice.
Incubate for 00:20:00 on a benchtop shaker (Pipette.com, Cat.#2231000667) set at Room temperature .
Collect liquid.
Repeat steps 18-20.
Add 20 µL, Acetonitrile . Incubate for 00:05:00 then collect liquid.
Use a SpeedVac (Fisher Scientific, Cat.#07-748-13) to dry the liquid completely.
Add 50 µL, Acetonitrile and incubate for 00:15:00 then remove liquid.
Collect liquid in a clean tube.
Buffers to prepare fresh: %s %s Store DTT in -20 °C and Trypsin in -80 °C . IAA is light sensitive. Store in amber tube (Fisher Scientific, Cat.#05-402-31).
After the tubes are completely dry in Step 24, add 17 µL, Buffer A then transfer to plastic autosampler vials (Agilent, Cat.#5182-0567,#5182-0564) or 96-well plate (Bio-Rad, Cat.#HSP9655).
Store at -20 °C until ready for LC-MS/MS analysis.
creator: Jennifer Gin
